---
title: "Principles of Spatial Data Visualization"

format:
  html:
    toc: true
  revealjs:
    output-file: slides-session2b-spatial-data-vis.html
    theme: [default, dmgt.scss]
    logo: dmgt_asia_logo.png
    footer: "DMgtAsia · Spatial Epidemiology"
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    slide-number: c/t
    show-slide-number: all
    progress: true
    transition: fade
    scrollable: true
    code-line-numbers: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    highlight-style: github
    incremental: false
    embed-resources: false
other-links:
  - text: "View as slides"
    href: slides-session2b-spatial-data-vis.html
    icon: easel2
---


## Spatial Data Visualization
### Cartographic Principles


-   Geography and Geospatial Science Working Group (GeoSWG) recognised the need for best practices in cartography.

    -   Visual contrast
    -   Legibility
    -   Figure-Ground Orientation
    -   Hierarchical Organization
    -   Balance

<br> These guidelines, help the researchers develop high-quality, consistent map products.

---

## Cartographic Principles (cont.)

![](images/designprinciples_2-lg.png)

---

## Cartographic Principles (examples)

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
![](images/designprinciples_3.jpg)

![](images/designprinciples_4.jpg)

![](images/designprinciples_5.jpg)

![](images/designprinciples_6.jpg)
:::


## Cartographic Guidelines for Public Health

::: columns
::: {.column width="40%"}
-   CDC, Atlanta
-   Some important aspects:
    -   Map Elements
        -   Title and Borders
        -   North Arrow / Graticule / Scale
        -   Inset Maps
        -   Labels and Legend
    -   Other Elements
        -   Data Sources
        -   Dates
        -   Projection
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}
![](images/map_elements_1.jpg){width="100%"}

<https://www.cdc.gov/dhdsp/maps/gisx/resources/cartographic_guidelines.pdf>
:::
:::
